findings.summary: WS2812B net resolution bug: VDD pin (pin 1) placed on 'DOUT' net; False positive rf_matching detection: R1 (470 ohm) + L1 (WS2812B LED) flagged as RF matching network

incorrect.description: In nets['DOUT'], pin_number '1' (pin_name 'VDD', pin_type 'power_in') of L1 is listed as a member of the DOUT signal net. This is wrong — VDD is a power pin that should be on +5V. The +5V net also separately lists L1 pin 2 (DOUT output) as a member, suggesting the pin-to-net assignment is swapped for L1. The root cause appears to be incorrect coordinate-based net matching in the legacy .sch parser when a mirrored component has closely spaced pins.

signal_analysis.rf_matching contains one entry with antenna='R1' (a 470-ohm series resistor for DIN) and components=[{ref:'L1', type:'inductor', value:'WS2812B'}]. R1 is a signal-line series resistor and WS2812B is an addressable LED, not an RF matching network. The rf_matching detector is triggering on any resistor adjacent to a component classified as 'inductor' regardless of context. This false positive also appears in the 20-pixel and 30-pixel ring schematics since WS2812B LEDs all get classified as 'inductor' type.

findings.summary: WS2812B LEDs classified as 'inductor' component type across all pixel ring schematics

incorrect.description: All 20 WS2812B instances in the 20-pixel ring (L1-L20), all 30 in the 30-pixel ring, and the one in WS2812B_Footprint_test are typed as 'inductor' in both statistics.component_types and the BOM. The schematic library uses reference prefix 'L' for these LEDs (a KiCad 5 library convention), and the analyzer classifies by prefix. WS2812B is an IC/LED, not an inductor. This causes component_types to show {inductor:20, capacitor:19} instead of the correct {led:20, capacitor:19}. The addressable_led_chains detector also produces [] empty despite 20 LEDs in a daisy-chain topology being present.

findings.summary: Multi-sheet schematic parsed correctly with 3 sheets, 112 components aggregated

correct.description: Top-level + two sub-sheets (20 pixel and 30 pixel rings) all resolved. Component counts (50 WS2812B + 48 caps + 2 resistors + 12 connectors = 112) match expected totals. Sheet file paths correctly captured in sheet_files array.

findings.summary: Empty template schematic correctly produces zero-component output

correct.description: An empty schematic (no components, no nets) correctly outputs total_components=0, empty BOM, empty signal_analysis sections. No false positives on an empty design.

findings.summary: board_width_mm reported as 0.0 for circular PCB; Courtyard overlaps, thermal zones, DFM tier correctly analyzed

correct.description: 46 footprints (40 SMD + 6 THT) on front side, 2 copper pour zones with thermal reliefs, courtyard overlap detection, and DFM tier='standard' all look reasonable for this design. routing_complete=true with 0 unrouted nets is plausible.

incorrect.description: The 20-pixel ring is a circular PCB (~50mm diameter based on gerber extents of ~49mm). The analyzer reports board_width_mm=0.0 and board_height_mm=25.0. The Edge.Cuts layer of circular boards uses arc/circle primitives rather than line segments, which the board outline parser apparently does not handle, yielding zero width. This same issue likely affects 30-pixel ring and nested ring PCBs. Confirmed: dfm.metrics also shows board_width_mm=0.0.

findings.summary: False positive misalignment warning on circular board: Edge.Cuts extent 10.5x0.0mm is wrong

incorrect.description: alignment.aligned=false with 'Width varies by 38.4mm across copper/edge layers'. The Edge.Cuts gerber shows width=10.5, height=0.0 — clearly wrong for a ~50mm circular board. The circular board outline is drawn as arc/circle commands in the Edge.Cuts gerber and the extent computation is failing to account for arc geometry, producing a degenerate bounding box. The misalignment warning is therefore a false positive driven by the same arc-handling gap.

findings.summary: complete=false reported despite all 9 required+recommended layers being present, because no drill file

incorrect.description: completeness shows all required layers found, missing_recommended=[], but complete=false and has_pth_drill=false/has_npth_drill=false. The design has 112 footprints with THT pads yet no drill file was exported into this gerber set. The 'complete' flag being false is technically correct (drill file missing), but the message should clarify that layers are complete but drill files are absent — the current output does not distinguish these failure modes clearly.

findings.summary: Simple 2-layer gerber set correctly analyzed with alignment, drill classification, and pad summary

correct.description: board_dimensions 13x10mm from Edge.Cuts, aligned=true, 2 vias (0.4mm) + 4 component holes (1.016mm) correctly classified, smd_ratio=0.0 (no SMD paste apertures, which is correct for this all-THT connector footprint). Completeness correct with F.Paste noted as missing_recommended.
